stem: A healthcare operations study measures the wait time (minutes) for a sample of 15 emergency room visitors. The sample mean is 356.3 min with a sample standard deviation of 133.7 min. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 336.7 min and standard deviation of 104.7 min. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 356.3, 95% CI = [282.2, 430.4]. Without outliers: mean = 336.7, 95% CI = [278.6, 394.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [278.6, 430.4]. Point estimate: 346.5 min.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [278.6, 430.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
